title: All Palindromes
description:
Given a string s (characters a–z), for each position i (0-indexed) compute the length of the longest palindromic substring that ends at i. Print n numbers separated by spaces.

Time limit: 1.00 s
Memory limit: 512 MB

examples.explanation: At each index, report the length of the longest palindrome ending there. For example, at index 2, "aba" has length 3; at index 7, "abababab" has length 8.

mcqChain.questionText: For input "a", what should be printed?

options.label: Depends on the previous character

mcqChain.explanation: Single character is always a palindrome of length 1 ending at that position.

wrongExplanations.1: Length cannot be 0 because the single character itself is a palindrome.

mcqChain.hint: Consider substrings ending at the only position.

mcqChain.questionText: For input "ab", what is the correct output?
mcqChain.explanation: At each index only the single character is a palindrome: lengths are 1 and 1.

wrongExplanations.0: "ab" is not a palindrome, so second position cannot be 2.

mcqChain.hint: Check palindromes ending at each index separately.

mcqChain.questionText: For input "aa", what is the output?
mcqChain.explanation: At index 0: 'a' → 1. At index 1: 'aa' → 2 is the longest ending there.

wrongExplanations.0: At index 1, "aa" is a palindrome of length 2.

mcqChain.hint: Check the substring ending at index 1: "aa".

mcqChain.questionText: For input "aba", what should be printed?
mcqChain.explanation: Ends: index 0 → 1, index 1 → 1 (only 'b'), index 2 → "aba" of length 3.

wrongExplanations.0: At index 1, the longest is 'b' of length 1, not 2.

mcqChain.hint: Check palindrome centered at index 1 for the last position.

mcqChain.questionText: For input "abba", choose the correct output.

wrongExplanations.2: At index 1, longest ending palindrome is 'b', not 'ab'.

mcqChain.hint: Even-length palindrome ends at index 2 and 3.

mcqChain.questionText: If for each position i you check all substrings that end at i to see if they are palindromes, what is the time complexity?

options.label: O(n log n)
O(n)

mcqChain.explanation: There are O(n) endings and O(n) candidates per ending, giving O(n^2).

wrongExplanations.2: You would re-check many substrings; linear time is not possible this way.

mcqChain.hint: Count how many substrings end at a given index across all indices.

mcqChain.questionText: Which statement helps reduce the work at each step i?

options.label: The longest palindrome ending at i is the longest palindromic suffix of s[0..i].
The longest palindrome ending at i must start at index 0.
The longest palindrome ending at i is always centered at i.
The longest palindrome ending at i must have even length.

mcqChain.explanation: Any palindrome ending at i is a suffix of the prefix s[0..i]. The longest such suffix is what we need.

wrongExplanations.2: Center can be left of i for even-length palindromes.

mcqChain.hint: Focus on palindromic suffixes of the prefix ending at i.

mcqChain.questionText: To avoid re-scanning many characters when s[i] is added, what should you maintain between palindromic suffixes?

options.label: A way to jump from a palindromic suffix to the next shorter palindromic suffix.
A list of all substrings of the prefix.
Only the last character seen so far.
Indices of all occurrences of each letter.

mcqChain.explanation: A direct jump from a palindrome to its longest proper palindromic suffix lets us test extensions quickly.

wrongExplanations.1: Storing all substrings is too large and doesn't help with quick jumps.

mcqChain.hint: Think about linking a palindrome to a shorter one that is also a suffix.

mcqChain.questionText: We will store nodes representing palindromic substrings and keep two special starting nodes. What lengths should these two special nodes have?

options.label: Lengths -1 and 0
Lengths 0 and 1
Lengths -2 and -1
Lengths 1 and 2

mcqChain.explanation: A node of length -1 and one of length 0 simplify boundary checks and handle first extensions.

wrongExplanations.1: Length 1 is an actual palindrome; we need a virtual base to always match.

mcqChain.hint: One should represent the empty case; the other should allow matching s[i] with itself.

mcqChain.questionText: Given a node representing a palindrome of length L that is a suffix of s[0..pos-1], what condition checks if it can be extended by s[pos] on both sides?
mcqChain.diagram:
Indexes: ... [pos-1-L] ... [pos-1] [pos]
We need s[pos-1-L] == s[pos] and that index exists.

options.label: pos - 1 - L >= 0 and s[pos - 1 - L] == s[pos]
pos - L >= 0 and s[pos - L] == s[pos]
pos - 1 - L >= 0 and s[pos - L] == s[pos]
pos + L + 1 < n and s[pos + 1] == s[pos - L]

mcqChain.explanation: We compare the new character s[pos] with the character just before the palindrome: s[pos-1-L].

wrongExplanations.1: This misses the -1 offset; it compares the wrong index.
wrongExplanations.2: Mismatch in index by one; must use pos-1-L.

mcqChain.hint: The left index is one before the current palindrome's start.

mcqChain.questionText: If the current longest palindromic suffix can't be extended at position pos, how do we find the next candidate quickly?

options.label: Follow a stored link to its longest proper palindromic suffix and test again; repeat until it works.
Restart from the longest node every time and test all lengths.
Move to a node with length L-1 without checking characters.
Binary search on lengths among all palindromic suffixes.

mcqChain.explanation: Jumping via links to the next shorter palindromic suffix avoids scanning many characters.

wrongExplanations.2: Length L-1 may not be a palindrome suffix; we need a valid palindromic suffix.

mcqChain.hint: Each node should know its next shorter palindromic suffix node.

mcqChain.questionText: When a palindrome of length L can be extended by s[pos], what is the length of the resulting palindrome?
mcqChain.diagram: Old palindrome: length L, add the same character on both ends.

options.label: Depends on previous character only

mcqChain.explanation: We add one character on each side, increasing the length by 2.

wrongExplanations.0: We are adding on both sides, so +2 not +1.

mcqChain.hint: You add one matching character to both ends.

mcqChain.questionText: When creating a new node for the extended palindrome at pos, how should its link be set?

options.label: If its length is 1, link to the node of length 0; otherwise, go from the current node’s link, find the next extendable node, then follow the transition by s[pos].
Always link it to the node of length -1.
Link to the current node unconditionally.
If its length is odd, link to length 1; if even, link to length 0.

mcqChain.explanation: Length 1 palindromes link to the empty node. Otherwise, find the longest proper palindromic suffix that can also be extended by s[pos], and use its transition.

wrongExplanations.1: Length -1 is only a virtual root; links should point to valid palindromic suffixes.

mcqChain.hint: Use the same extend-checking process starting from the current node’s link.

mcqChain.questionText: After adding/finding the node for position pos, what should we output for this position?

options.label: The length stored in the 'last' node after processing pos.
The length of the current node before extension.
pos + 1
The length stored in the link of the 'last' node.

mcqChain.explanation: The 'last' node represents the longest palindromic suffix ending at pos; its length is the answer.

wrongExplanations.2: pos+1 is the whole prefix length, not necessarily a palindrome.

mcqChain.hint: Track the node for the longest suffix palindrome as 'last'.

mcqChain.questionText: What should each node’s transition map (by character) represent?

options.label: For a character c, the palindrome obtained by adding c to both ends of this node’s palindrome, if it exists.
For a character c, the palindrome obtained by appending c to the right only.
For a character c, the next node in input order.
For a character c, the previous node visited when processing.

mcqChain.explanation: We only create transitions for palindromes formed by wrapping with the same character on both sides.

wrongExplanations.1: Appending on one side doesn’t generally keep a palindrome.

mcqChain.hint: Extensions must stay palindromic by mirroring the same character.

mcqChain.questionText: Why is the special node of length -1 useful?

options.label: It guarantees the first character can always be matched with itself without extra boundary checks.
It stores negative length palindromes for later use.
It represents all even-length palindromes.
It speeds up printing the results.

mcqChain.explanation: With length -1, the check s[pos-1-(-1)] == s[pos] reduces to s[pos]==s[pos], always true when pos≥0.

wrongExplanations.2: Even-length palindromes are represented by normal nodes, not a single special node.

mcqChain.hint: Think about how to avoid pos-1-L going negative on the first character.

mcqChain.questionText: Read the string and its length efficiently.

options.label:
s = sys.stdin.readline()
n = len(s)

s = input().split()
n = len(s)

import sys
s = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
n = len(s)

s = input()
n = int(s)

mcqChain.explanation: Strip the newline to avoid counting it as a character. Then take len(s).

wrongExplanations.0: Without strip, the newline is included and breaks results.
wrongExplanations.1: split() returns a list, not the string.

mcqChain.hint: Be careful with trailing newlines.
mcqChain.codeSnippet:
import sys
s = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
n = len(s)

mcqChain.questionText: Define a node to store a palindrome’s length, a link to a shorter palindromic suffix, and transitions.
mcqChain.explanation: We need len, link, and a dict for transitions keyed by characters.

wrongExplanations.1: Field name mismatch: later code expects 'len' and 'next'.
wrongExplanations.3: We need to set fields based on inputs length and link.

mcqChain.hint: Transitions map characters to node indices.

mcqChain.questionText: Initialize the structure with two special nodes.

options.label:
nodes = []
nodes.append(Node(0, 0))
nodes.append(Node(1, 0))

nodes = []
nodes.append(Node(-1, 0))
nodes.append(Node(0, 0))

nodes = []
nodes.append(Node(-1, -1))
nodes.append(Node(0, -1))

nodes = []
nodes.append(Node(0, 1))
nodes.append(Node(-1, 0))

mcqChain.explanation: Create nodes of lengths -1 and 0. Their links can both be 0 initially.

wrongExplanations.0: Lengths should be -1 and 0 to serve as virtual bases.

mcqChain.hint: First node length -1, second length 0.

mcqChain.questionText: Set the starting 'last' node and the answer array.

options.label:
last = 1
ans = [0] * n

last = 0
ans = [0] * n

last = 1
ans = [0] * (n + 1)

last = 2
ans = [0] * n

mcqChain.explanation: The longest palindromic suffix of the empty prefix is the length-0 node (index 1). ans has n entries.

wrongExplanations.1: Index 0 is the -1 length node; last should start at length 0 node.

mcqChain.hint: Use the empty palindrome node as the current suffix.

mcqChain.questionText: Implement a function to jump to the longest palindromic suffix that can be extended by s[pos].
mcqChain.explanation: We must loop following links until the extend condition with pos-1-l holds.

wrongExplanations.1: Off-by-one in the index check; must be pos-1-l.
wrongExplanations.2: Needs to loop; a single step may not reach an extendable palindrome.

mcqChain.hint: Compare s[pos] with the character just before the palindrome.

mcqChain.questionText: Iterate over the string and find the current extendable node and character.
mcqChain.explanation: We process every index i, find cur with get_link(last, i), and set ch = s[i].
mcqChain.hint: We pass the current 'last' and the position i.

mcqChain.questionText: Handle the case when the transition by ch exists or create a new node and edge.
mcqChain.explanation: If the child already exists, move to it. Otherwise create a new node of length +2, set now to last index, and record the transition from cur.

wrongExplanations.2: Length should increase by 2 when wrapping both sides.
wrongExplanations.3: len(nodes) is out-of-range as an index; last index is len(nodes)-1.

mcqChain.hint: New palindrome length is old length + 2, and now is the last appended index.

mcqChain.questionText: Set the new node’s link correctly, update last, store the answer, and print.

options.label:
if nodes[now].len == 1:
        nodes[now].link = 1
    else:
        link_candidate = nodes[cur].link
        link_candidate = get_link(link_candidate, i)
        nodes[now].link = nodes[link_candidate].next[ch]
    last = now
ans[i] = nodes[last].len
print(" ".join(str(x) for x in ans))

if nodes[now].len == 1:
        nodes[now].link = 0
    else:
        link_candidate = cur
        link_candidate = get_link(link_candidate, i)
        nodes[now].link = nodes[link_candidate].link
    last = now
ans[i] = nodes[cur].len
print("".join(str(x) for x in ans))

if nodes[now].len == 1:
        nodes[now].link = 1
    else:
        nodes[now].link = 0
    last = now
ans[i] = nodes[cur].len
print(" ".join(map(str, ans)))

if nodes[now].len == 1:
        nodes[now].link = 1
    else:
        link_candidate = nodes[cur].link
        link_candidate = get_link(link_candidate, i)
        nodes[now].link = link_candidate
    last = now
ans[i] = nodes[last].link
print(" ".join(str(x) for x in ans))

mcqChain.explanation: Length-1 palindromes link to the empty node (index 1). Otherwise find the extendable suffix from cur’s link and follow its transition by ch. Answer is nodes[last].len.

wrongExplanations.1: The link should be a node reached by a transition from the extendable suffix, not its link.
wrongExplanations.3: ans[i] should be the length of last, not its link index.

mcqChain.hint: Use the same character transition from the extendable suffix to set the link.
mcqChain.codeSnippet:
if nodes[now].len == 1:
            nodes[now].link = 1
        else:
            link_candidate = nodes[cur].link
            link_candidate = get_link(link_candidate, i)
            nodes[now].link = nodes[link_candidate].next[ch]
        last = now
    ans[i] = nodes[last].len
print(" ".join(str(x) for x in ans))

mcqChain.questionText: What does the program print for input "ababb"?
mcqChain.hint: Check palindromic suffix at each index.

mcqChain.questionText: For input "ababbababaa", what is the value printed at index 7 (0-based)?
mcqChain.diagram: Indices: 0..10; at index 7 the prefix is "ababbaba".
mcqChain.explanation: At index 7, the longest palindromic suffix is "abababab" of length 8.

wrongExplanations.0: There exists a longer even-length palindrome ending at 7.
wrongExplanations.3: Odd-length palindromes are shorter here than 8.

mcqChain.hint: Consider even-length palindrome wrapping around the center between indices 3 and 4.

mcqChain.questionText: For input "aaaaa", what is the value at index 3?
mcqChain.explanation: The longest palindrome ending at index 3 is "aaaa" of length 4.

wrongExplanations.0: Longer even-length palindrome exists ending at 3.

mcqChain.hint: Repeated characters allow long even-length palindromes.

mcqChain.questionText: What is the time complexity of processing the entire string with this approach?

options.label: O(n)
O(n log n)
O(n + number of substrings)

mcqChain.explanation: Each position adds at most one node, and link traversals are amortized O(1). Overall O(n).

wrongExplanations.2: We avoid checking all substrings via links and transitions.

mcqChain.hint: Each character leads to a bounded number of link traversals and possibly one node creation.

mcqChain.questionText: What is the space complexity of the data structure in the worst case?

options.label: O(n)
O(n log n)

mcqChain.explanation: There are at most O(n) distinct palindromic substrings plus two roots; maps store at most O(n) edges.

wrongExplanations.1: We store many nodes and transitions as the string grows.

mcqChain.hint: How many distinct palindromic substrings can a string have?

finalCodeSnippets:
import sys
s = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
n = len(s)

            nodes[now].link = 1
        else:
            link_candidate = nodes[cur].link
            link_candidate = get_link(link_candidate, i)
            nodes[now].link = nodes[link_candidate].next[ch]
        last = now
    ans[i] = nodes[last].len
print(" ".join(str(x) for x in ans))

reasoningChain.question: Which statement helps reduce the work at each step i?
reasoningChain.insight: The answer at i is the longest palindromic suffix of the prefix s[0..i].

reasoningChain.question: To avoid re-scanning many characters when s[i] is added, what should you maintain between palindromic suffixes?
reasoningChain.insight: Link each palindrome to its longest proper palindromic suffix to enable quick jumps.

reasoningChain.question: We will store nodes representing palindromic substrings and keep two special starting nodes. What lengths should these two special nodes have?
reasoningChain.insight: Use virtual nodes of length -1 and 0 to simplify boundary cases and first extensions.

reasoningChain.question: Given a node representing a palindrome of length L that is a suffix of s[0..pos-1], what condition checks if it can be extended by s[pos] on both sides?
reasoningChain.insight: Check s[pos-1-L] == s[pos] with boundary test; this finds extendable palindromic suffixes.

reasoningChain.question: When creating a new node for the extended palindrome at pos, how should its link be set?
reasoningChain.insight: If new length is 1, link to length 0; otherwise, from the current link find an extendable suffix and use its transition.

reasoningChain.question: After adding/finding the node for position pos, what should we output for this position?
reasoningChain.insight: The 'last' node represents the longest palindrome ending at pos; its length is the answer.
